message:
Last week my 8-year-old asked why we have flashlights in the hall closet. I said, 'For when the power goes out during a storm.' She goes, 'Oh, like when you forget to pay the electric bill?' 😅 

It made me realize—my kids have *no idea* why we do half this stuff. They just see the supplies and assume the worst. So I spent an afternoon actually *showing* them: where the water jugs live, why we keep batteries together, how to use the weather radio. Turned it into a little treasure hunt instead of a scary lecture.

Now my son's in charge of 'battery patrol' and my daughter wants to be the 'flashlight inspector.' They're not anxious—they're just... ready. And honestly, so am I.

Do your kids know where the emergency stuff is, or is it more of a 'grown-up secret'?
